title: Federal Civil Rights Enforcement Act
description: Grants the Justice Department expanded authority to enforce desegregation and anti-discrimination law against states resisting compliance, with federal marshals authorized to intervene where local enforcement fails.
flavor: The Constitution doesn't have an asterisk for states that disagree with it.

effects.description: Passive: -1 unrest/mo for remainder of term

attorney_general.quote: The Attorney General calls it 'the tool this department has needed for a decade.'

protest_leader.quote: The movement calls it 'proof that marching in the streets still moves Washington.'

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader warns of 'federal overreach into matters best left to the states.'

title: Voting Rights Protection Act
description: Bars discriminatory voter registration practices nationwide and establishes federal oversight of registration procedures in jurisdictions with a documented history of suppression.
flavor: A right you can't exercise isn't a right. It's a promise, and promises aren't self-enforcing.

effects.description: Passive: +1 approval/mo for remainder of term

prereqLabel: Requires the Federal Civil Rights Enforcement Act to pass first

attorney_general.quote: The Attorney General calls it 'the natural next step after enforcement.'

protest_leader.quote: The movement calls it 'the follow-through we were promised.'

senate_leader.quote: The Senate Leader says it 'closes a gap that should have closed generations ago.'

title: National Interstate and Defense Highway Act
description: Funds a nationwide interstate highway network justified on both economic and military-mobility grounds — moving goods, workers, and if necessary, troops and equipment, faster than ever before.
flavor: Sold as national defense. Built as the backbone of the modern economy.

effects.description: Passive: +2 economy/mo for remainder of term

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary calls it 'infrastructure that pays for itself many times over.'

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense notes 'the mobility value alone justifies the investment.'

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'the rare bill every district wants a piece of.'

title: National Space Exploration Act
description: Establishes a dedicated civilian space agency with authority and funding to pursue an ambitious program of orbital and eventual crewed deep-space missions.
flavor: The high ground of the next century isn't a hill. It's an orbit.

effects.description: Passive: +1 global reputation/mo for remainder of term

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense sees 'obvious dual-use value in the technology this program will produce.'

foreign_ally.quote: PM Faircross calls it 'a program the whole alliance can take pride in.'

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary warns the price tag 'will be felt for a generation.'

title: National Satellite Communications Act
description: Funds development of a satellite communications network for both civilian broadcast use and secure military command-and-control — dual-purpose infrastructure for the space age.
flavor: Whoever owns the sky owns the conversation.

effects.description: Passive: +1 security/mo for remainder of term

prereqLabel: Requires the National Space Exploration Act to pass first

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense calls it 'command-and-control infrastructure two decades overdue.'

media_anchor.quote: Anchor Sato notes 'broadcast reach like this changes how the whole country gets its news.'

title: National Defense Education Act
description: Provides federal funding for science, mathematics, and language education at every level, framed explicitly as a response to falling behind rivals in trained scientists and engineers.
flavor: The next war might be won or lost in a high school classroom.

effects.description: Passive: +1 economy/mo for remainder of term

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary calls it 'an investment that compounds for thirty years.'

speaker.quote: The Speaker notes 'not a single district objects to more scientists and engineers.'

title: Elderly and Disabled Healthcare Act
description: Establishes a federal health insurance program for Americans over 65 and those with qualifying disabilities, financed through a combination of payroll contributions and general revenue.
flavor: A lifetime of paying in should mean something when you can no longer work.

effects.description: Passive: +2 approval/mo for remainder of term

protest_leader.quote: Director Marsh calls it 'the kind of bill people remember at the ballot box for a generation.'

first_spouse.quote: First Spouse Hayes says 'every family I meet on the road has a story about why this matters.'

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader calls it 'a spending commitment the Treasury cannot sustain.'

title: Strategic Arms Limitation Ratification Act
description: Ratifies a negotiated arms limitation treaty with the rival superpower, capping the growth of both nations' strategic arsenals and establishing mutual verification procedures.
flavor: The only arms race anyone truly wins is the one that never has to be run.

effects.description: Passive: +1 global reputation/mo for remainder of term

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense warns 'verification procedures on paper are not the same as security in practice.'

foreign_adversary.quote: Premier Rostov calls it 'proof that both our nations can still choose reason over ruin.'

senate_leader.quote: The Senate Leader calls it 'the treaty this chamber has waited a generation to ratify.'

title: Strategic Forces Modernization Act
description: Authorizes a major expansion and modernization of strategic forces, funding next-generation delivery systems and expanded production capacity across the defense industrial base.
flavor: Peace through strength only works if the strength is real.

effects.description: Passive: +2 military readiness/mo for remainder of term

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense calls it 'the modernization the Joint Chiefs have requested for years.'

foreign_adversary.quote: Premier Rostov calls it 'a provocation dressed up as prudence.'

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary flags 'a bill this size will define the budget for a decade.'

title: Peaceful Atomic Energy Development Act
description: Funds civilian nuclear power research and development, subsidizing the construction of the first generation of commercial reactors and positioning atomic energy as a peacetime dividend of the nuclear age.
flavor: The same science that built the bomb can also light a city.

effects.description: Passive: +1 economy/mo for remainder of term

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary calls it 'cheap power that pays dividends for decades.'

protest_leader.quote: Director Marsh raises 'real questions about safety that deserve more than a press release.'

title: Strategic Grain Reserve Act
description: Establishes a federally managed strategic grain reserve, stabilizing farm income against price swings while giving the government a reserve to draw on for both emergencies and diplomacy.
flavor: Food is a weapon whether or not you choose to use it that way.

effects.description: Passive: +1 economy/mo for remainder of term

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary calls it 'stability farm-state economies have wanted for years.'

speaker.quote: The Speaker notes 'this one clears the House without much of a fight.'

title: Federal and Defense Worker Pension Act
description: Expands and guarantees pension protections for federal employees and defense-industry workers, many of whom have spent careers in classified or hazardous roles without adequate retirement security.
flavor: The people who kept the secrets deserve more than a handshake on the way out.

effects.description: Passive: -1 unrest/mo for remainder of term

protest_leader.quote: Director Marsh calls it 'overdue recognition for workers this country asked a lot of.'

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense notes 'morale in the defense workforce genuinely depends on this kind of security.'

title: Continental Early Warning and Air Defense Act
description: Funds a continent-spanning network of early-warning radar stations and interceptor bases, designed to give a decisive head start on detecting and responding to a bomber or missile attack.
flavor: A warning system is only as good as the minutes it buys you. This one is built to buy as many as physics allows.

effects.description: Passive: +1 security/mo for remainder of term

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense calls it 'the minutes of warning that make every other defense plan possible.'

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary notes 'this is a permanent budget line, not a one-time cost.'

title: Federal Employment Loyalty Review Reform Act
description: Replaces sweeping, vaguely worded loyalty oath requirements for federal employment with narrower, evidence-based review procedures — aimed at protecting national security without ending careers on rumor and guilt by association.
flavor: Security and due process were never actually supposed to be opposites.

effects.description: Passive: -1 unrest/mo for remainder of term

attorney_general.quote: The Attorney General calls it 'due process this department should have insisted on years ago.'

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader warns it 'ties the hands of the people trying to keep this government secure.'

title: Strategic Minerals Stockpile Act
description: Establishes a federal stockpile of minerals and materials critical to defense manufacturing — titanium, cobalt, and rare industrial alloys — insulating war production from a supply cutoff by a hostile or unstable source.
flavor: You can't build a deterrent out of a supply chain someone else controls.

effects.description: Passive: +1 security/mo for remainder of term

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary calls it 'cheap insurance against a supply shock we can't afford to be surprised by.'

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense notes 'production doesn't stop for a war if the materials never stop flowing.'

title: National Radiation Health Monitoring Act
description: Establishes federal monitoring of radiation exposure near weapons testing and production sites, along with a public health registry for affected communities and workers.
flavor: The people living downwind didn't get a vote on the testing schedule. They should at least get a doctor who knows why they're sick.

effects.description: Passive: +1 approval/mo for remainder of term

protest_leader.quote: Director Marsh calls it 'the first time this government has admitted downwind communities were owed anything at all.'

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense worries the registry 'creates a paper trail for questions this department would rather not answer.'

title: Cold War Veterans Readjustment Act
description: Expands education, housing, and mental health benefits for servicemembers returning from extended overseas deployments — many from conflicts the public barely acknowledges as wars at all.
flavor: They served in a war nobody at home wanted to call a war. The benefits shouldn't be quiet about it either.

effects.description: Passive: -1 unrest/mo for remainder of term

protest_leader.quote: Director Marsh calls it 'the least this country can do for what it asked of them.'

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense says 'every servicemember I talk to already knows someone this would help.'

title: Advanced Computing Research Initiative Act
description: Funds a dedicated federal research program in advanced computing and cryptography, aimed at maintaining a decisive technical edge in code-breaking, simulation, and command-and-control systems.
flavor: The next advantage won't be measured in warheads. It'll be measured in how fast you can compute.

effects.description: Passive: +1 economy/mo for remainder of term

sec_defense.quote: The Secretary of Defense calls it 'the codebreaking and simulation edge this department will need for the next generation of threats.'

treasury_secretary.quote: The Treasury Secretary notes 'basic research like this has a way of paying for itself decades later.'
